A JavaScript test script with a loop that increments by 5 until it reaches 100.

# Purpose
This code is a simple loop that increments a variable `i` by 5 in each iteration. The loop continues indefinitely until `i` equals 100, at which point the `break` statement terminates the loop. The code demonstrates a basic use of a `for` loop with an empty condition, relying on the `break` statement to exit the loop when a specific condition is met. This script provides narrow functionality, primarily serving as an example of loop control and termination in JavaScript.
